analysis.name: Terri Anderson

analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 43 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment:
She's the worst. Forces students through lockdown browser quizzes riddled with ultra-specific gotcha questions. Hides behind vague grading criteria. L professor. Deducts points at random with zero explanation. Overrides TAs grades and fights with them. btw she hides in the TA GroupMe
TLDR: TAs made the class- professors meh.

I'm writing this review because I don't want others to go through what I did. She lacks basic compassion and treats students with an unjustified level of suspicion. I hope this helps the next student make a better choice.
Very bad experience for this professor, the lectures are interesting but I believe she could handle student concerns in a much more supportive and effective manner.

Avoid this class. Prof is strict and unapproachable, with random grade deductions and no feedback. Requires Doc link history for assignments and checks for AI usage. Weekly quizzes are overly specific. No chance to challenge grades or get help via email. Heavy reading and videos. Midterm essay and Oral Final. Weekly discussion, quiz, and perusal.

The other comments aren't lying this class is hard because there's too many assignments due every week. Theres a lot of reading and the weekly quizzes are one attempt only and not specific enough to what you read. The hardest class I've taken at ucla solely because of the workload. I got really burnt out in the first four weeks.

analysis.name: Ceasar Ayala
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Material is manageable, but the professor is  is dismissive of student concerns and often redirects questions to ChatGPT rather than providing instruction. Lectures frequently end in 20 mins, making commutes feel wasted.
Bro just read off the slides, I'd rather open my YouTube watch someone else explain the concepts, or let the TA do the job. Yes he has accent, be prepare for that

He talks really fast, can barely understand him. But, he posts his readings/lecture notes online - very helpful. If it wasn't for the T.A. though, I'd probably do horribly.. so go to discussion! Tests are hard so study hard! Fill in the blanks and short answer.. fill in the blanks are very hard.

He speaks really fast and has an accent, but you will get use to it by week 3. Very knowledgeable with his stuff and is honest if he feels ambivalent with a concept. All readings are available on class website so didn't have to buy anything! Except an iclicker used during lecture for questions. & no surprises on tests! Cool guy!

I took professor Ayala in the winter of 2016. Class was straight forward.  2 page paper, midterm, and final. This class is very dense. A lot of reading but it's sociological theory so it's bound to be dense. I would take him again.

analysis.name: Saba Aliyari
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This class was so much harder than it should have been (worser than actual upper lvl in person classes) ! There were no clear guidelines ever and lectures were so incredibly dry. Tests were worth so much with each question being worth like 9 points each as a mcq, and every question was an application question with multiple parts and pathways.

I absolutely loved Professor Saba Aliyari. She is an amazing teacher who encouraged me every step of the way, offered TA hours on the weekends, stayed on after lectures to answer questions, and was generally excellent in every possible way.
Nice the nicest professor and the class is a lot of work, but if you keep up with the homework and do the extra credit, you'll be OK and you'll learn a lot

There's a ton of work to do, but you learn a lot- you do a lot of the work outside of class and come to lectures for solidifying the learning through participation. She gives questions that are thought-provoking w/ irl context which I think would be helpful for the MCAT. Gives extra credit opportunities that are useful. Took LS7A.

She is smart and very passionate on what she does, but she is not a good lecturer. It was hard to understand - her accent was strong, and lecture is not organized. Hard to focus on lecture. She wants your participation but she wants you to explain what you didn't learn yet. This was 100 - but you have to review science paper. HUH??!?

analysis.name: Samy Alim
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: Great professor and very passionate about the subject. There are 2 papers & exams along with weekly discussion posts based on the readings. The class is mainly highlighting main points of the reading and this is what will be on the quiz. I recommend going to office hours as that will give you more insight into what will be on the exams.

He's not that bad as the people below said. Yes it is true the readings end up being a waste of time, hard to understand, and so long, but he makes up for it on his exams. I barely glanced over my notes the night before the midterm and still got an A on it (all you have to do is throw in terms and your answers will be fine)

This class sucks!!! I have never read so much in my life only to retain such a minimal amount of information. Dont take this class unless you want to persue a PdD in Linguistics which is pretty much what you need in order to understand the material.
most UNORGANIZED class I've ever taken in college.  reading is assigned as if its the only class you're taking.

Despite the other reviews, I really enjoyed this class. Professor Alim is very passionate when he's teaching. I thought the class was very easy and pretty interesting. I also didn't bother buying the book we were supposed to get, and we didn't even end up using it. He also gives EC opportunities which is nice.

analysis.name: Aaron Burke
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Do not take this class for a GE. Trust me. It is not worth your time. Take your GE's at a CC and transfer them over. Figuring out how to drive is easier than this class. Unnecessarily rude professor and TA's, extreme amount of reading, balancing this class is not possible. Get ready to read and get ready to stress out. AVOID THIS CLASS.

He's a descent guy and a descent professor. 3 papers overall, midterm, & final exam. Reading outside material helps a lot and office hours with TA. Definitely a doable class. Crammed for class the night before the midterm and pulled off a 92, not knowing any of the info when I started.

Clear and interesting lectures. No textbook, all pdf articles. 1 written midterm (short answers) and same with final. Cumulative. Weekly reflection papers based on the lectures and "key terms" the student creates from them. Once I stopped using the articles for the basis of my reflection papers and solely used lecture notes, I got better grades.

His lectures aren't terrible but it definitely feels like it's missing something. Most of the information I get from the course comes from my TA, Jacob, or from reading over the extra material- not so much the actual textbook. There is one midterm and a final. Also, two 3 page papers and one 7 page paper.

analysis.name: Navid Amini
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed this course! It was focused mainly on Tableau, and we learned a lot of hands-on skills that I can actually use. Amini was fair and organized, grading was always done quickly, and there were plenty of opportunities to earn extra. Overall, a great class for anyone interested in learning data visualization in a practical way.

Professor Navid is by far my favorite professor at CSULA! His lectures are extremely thorough and interesting, and projects are actually fun instead of the boring things that are usually of the book. I am having no problems with homework, but the quizzes are HARD! You will have to study up for them. After this class, you will know your stuff.

I dropped this class. This class was taken online and was supposed to be on Tuesdays and Thursdays. However, professor Amini only showed up on Tuesdays and did not teach anything, he only went over what slides, videos, and textbook material we should be learning. No teaching was done, expectations are high, expect to hand write code &teach yourself

Professor Amini is a great instructor, he clearly explains the subject well. If you attend lectures and labs and review them you should be good. This class is more about how much you practice and understand Java.
Prof. Amini really cares that we learn. The material was very well organized and the lectures were amazing. I would definitely take any course he teaches!

review_highlights.comment: :) I love this prof so mush. He teaches sooooo well. And I think his lectures are very interesting.

The most terrible professor in UCLA. No idea what he is trying to teach in this course. He always talks to himself instead of to students. Tough grader and TAs. Deduction point without no reason even though that your homework are same as the homework who got the full point. When you try to appeal, good luck, since no one will reply.

he is a very interesting and funny teacher, he likes to make fun of himself. he is good in connecting real-life happenings in economics in class. and therefore, he makes it more interesting.  good teacher and i recommend him. the only part that he could increase a little is his knowledge of engli
He may be a good professor for graduate students, but he tries not to "waste" his time with undergraduate students. His lecture is okay.
He is okay teacher. Not easy, not hard. So, if you study at least sometime, you'll do fine in his classes.

analysis.name: Blake Allmendinger
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He is a nice guy but is disorganized. He will change assigned readings or turn papers into projects. Syllabus is highly subjected to change so if you like to get ahead on your work for the week, this will suck. If you show up to class and raise your hand he won't be a pain. His personality is annoying, he complains, and can be snarky and petty.

Prof. Allmendinger is professional but also funny & relaxed; you will look forward to each class if you read & appreciate witty humor -lectures are interesting & relate to real-life.  Smart, helps you to become a better writer instead of simply going through the motions. TRULY CARES, there for students, responds promptly to emails even on weekends.

Overall, Professor Allmendinger is very nice and extremely funny, however, he is not organized especially when it comes to explaining what he expects for papers and exams! Almost 20% of my class failed the midterm and it seems to be because he did not explain what he expected! If you take his class, be VERY careful and go to office hours!!

Your grade is set from your look. Seemed to anger him if you are cute & smart;he played "games" giving instructions then grading harshly for power "play".He likes All-American,UCLA types(Teaches the West!).If you are European or Ethnic,safely use convention and get low B, C, or D. It is hard to fail you if always use Standard writing conventions.

Detective Fiction was one of the best courses I've taken in my college career.  The subject matter is fascinating and entertaining and the lectures are interesting.  However, the tests are extremely difficult and require creativity and a mastery of the concepts discussed in class in addition to your own theories and ideas.  Tough class but worth it

analysis.name: Brent Corbin
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Great teacher.  Great person.  Great lectures.  Probably the best lower division professor you'll have for science majors.
Do not take him, rude professor; homework does not help at all with tests; extremely hard tests.

Great lecturer: organized and entertaining. The book is a reference, exams are based on lecture material. His tests are impossible to finish within 50min, but the final is way more doable. Just go to his office hours as much as you can because he goes over problems that help your solving skills during exams. Great person outside the classroom too!

Corbin excels in physics teaching. His dedication shines in office hours, ensuring deep understanding over memorization. Inspiring and thorough, he makes physics engaging and accessible. A true gem for students seeking to truly grasp the subject. The funniest parts is when he cracks jokes. For example, when deriving planar symmetry - old mcdonald.
Amazing professor, tests are really hard, but he explains the material very well, and is always available if you need help. Just go to office hours and show him that you care and want to learn.

analysis.name: Bonnie Goff
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took 1 class with her and she was I really liked how she spoked clearly.

Taking Professor Goff was probably my best choice I had made in UCLA. Extremely funny, clear, flexible, and understanding professor. During the finals week where WHO declared Covid 19 Pandemic, she was extremely quick and flexible with final even with such short notice. There is no required material and I personally think she grades fairly.

I want to warn everyone do not take any methods class from her. I took psych188. I went to see her during office hours three times, she was never there. Her assignments are extremely long and vague. half or more of class dropped, and I wish I had. I'm terrified I won't pass.

Professor Goff is great! You really learn valuable things in the class and she makes the material easy to understand. The class is pretty structured so you have to work hard but it's very doable. Highly recommend her!
One of the best psych professors at ucla. She makes her lectures very interesting funny and easy to follow.

analysis.name: Christina Bauer
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Amazing professor i took her at Cerritos, if you're intimidated by chemistry, Professor Bauer will be there for you. She makes Science fun. Attendance is required but I promise you wouldn't want to miss her class. Enjoy

She's a really nice professor. Did demonstrations in class a lot to try and excite people. Her tests were challenging though. I am pretty awful with the 20B focus on g-chem, and I tried really hard and failed the midterms and final, and got a C+, so yay. She says her class isn't meant to be a weeder class, so try and you will pass and learn.

She was really nice.  She wanted her students to learn the material and made the grade less important, which was nice.  The midterms are more difficult than the final and she is there to help you understand the material.  The only thing, was her study guides weren't very comprehensive of the test, so it was a bit difficult at first.

I can't give Dr. Bauer enough praise, she is kind, funny, passionate about Chemistry and puts the students before any grade. She is the definition of a Professor who cares about her students and making sure we actually learn the material instead of docking points and making University even harder than it is. Her love for science radiates

Professor Bauer is amazing! Highly recommend taking her class! She is always there when you need help or have questions. She makes the class easy! She does give a lot of work throughout the week but it is not hard at all. If you want a good chemistry lesson please don't hesitate to take her class!

analysis.name: Brennan Patrick
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward

He called me 5 minutes later to tell me he closed out my Grade and exempted me from the remaining work. I doubt he realized just  how much that kindness meant to me.

I took BIB1001 Old Testament History with Dr Patrick in spring term 2024
 ( PLNU ).He is a fast speaker but his lectures are recorded so you can listen a couple times if you need to.The downside of his class was that you only have 4 items that make up the entire grade so you can't afford to have a bad midterm or final and expect to pass .

Equal parts Arrogance & Ego + visibly bored in every lecture. That is what you get with this guy.His expectations are unrealistic&the grading scale is built around the assumption everyone taking this class is already an expert.

Doctor Patrick is that rare breed of professor where he is equally  as obnoxious as he is intellectually gifted. The man literally quoted HIMSELF in a lecture about his own published material . YES he pulls sections of the material from HIS OWN text. Unfortunately he is the only professor who teaches this course most years.

Law 675:HIV-AIDS Law and Public Policy(2019)

Dr.Patrick has an insane amount of knowledge on the subject matter which he somehow squeezes into a single term.I learned more from this course about the history of HIV-AIDS & LGBTQ Public-Judicial Policy than I have learned in 3 years of academic courses on the same subject.This was a fantastic class.

analysis.name: Zsuzsa Berend
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style

review_highlights.comment: If you like seminars and find the topic interesting DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. To call this a seminar is a joke, she conducts the "seminar" like verbal quizzes, checking if you remember what author said on pg3 and gushing about her reading list. She has ZERO tolerance to students having their own take, insights, or ideas. Her interpretation is law.
Her accent is really heavy, can not understand what is she actually saying. Really bad professor since she doesn't care about the student.

No one can understand what the professor is saying. She seems to be going on and on about nothing in particular. At the beginning of each lecture, the TA's pass out outlines for the class, but you can still never understand where we're at in the lecture because you can't tell what she's saying. I was really excited to take this class, but hate it!

I've learned a lot from her.  If you take the Honors Seminar, she is incredibly supportive, but don' expect sugar coating. You don't always have to agree with her, but she tries really hard to be honest with her students, which is preferable to vague feedback. Also, she makes herself really available, so I would recommend going to office hours.

Her accent is not that bad. No term paper. Midterm and final(30 points each). 100 to earn.  4 quizzes (5 each). Reaction papers (5 each). Her lectures are interesting some times. She provides a lecture outline. It's a pretty easy class. Just do the readings and make sure you go to office hours. She likes to know that you're trying. Really nice!

analysis.name: Clark Barrett
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: These lectures were amazing- I even saved the podcasts onto my computer to listen to again. Prof Barrett is speaks with incredible clarity, is kind, respectful and helpful in office hours, and posts all his slideshows before class and podcasts after class so you have everything you could possibly need to understand the concepts. The exams are fair.

Easy class just insanely boring. The whole thing consists of weekly reflections of 200 words, 3 quizzes 30 mins with 20 qs, and one final paper that you can choose what to write. The first 5 weeks were ok but I think as the class gets more complicated = he rambles more. It feels like I want to fall asleep through this class.

He is super smart, and the material is interesting. I don't think the tests are too hard - they are just really good at assessing how well you know the material. Considering how light the reading, and the load is; there is no excuse for you not to.

Prof Barrett is really nice. He posts all his lectures online so you theoretically don't ever have to go to the class. Exams are hard though, questions are worded in a confusing matter so make sure to do all the reading and either attend or watch the lectures. Don't get behind just because they're online!
There is no discussion section. All lectures are podcasted. Overall, his lectures are pretty boring, but his tests are fair. Be sure to study his lecture slides and you'll do fine.

Great Teacher! Fantastic! Very down to earth and approachable! She does extremely well in teaching Korean. She also teaches at L.A.C.C. so she always well prepares non-heritage speakers! A+
She is so nice!!!I loved her class!!The quizes were little bit hard for non-heritage speakers but she was very helpful to those students, too.

Wadie is truly the best TA (not only in French but at UCLA). As a senior, this is the first time I've ever posted on this site and I think that speaks to just how gifted Wadie is. I had rough quarter when I took French 2 and in many cases, my day was uplifted by just knowing that I had French with Wadie! An absolute gift to UCLA!
He is a very talented lecturer. He emphasizes practice problems, which is very helpful.

Genuinely might be the best professor I've learned from at UCLA. He subbed in for a class that I was pretty lost in and I was basically completely caught back up after the 3 lectures he taught. He's really good at explaining engineering concepts without just throwing endless formulas at you. This guy makes me wanna become a teacher someday.
Very example oriented, which is helpful with understanding statistics materials.

She expects you to remember some random name she threw out that won't be in any reading materials as it will likely be on a test. incredibly disorganized, doesn't take criticism well, hardly will even work with the TA's to help you

I don't think I've ever seen an ostensible professional, in a professional setting, act so unprofessionally. At the end of this farce of a class, when we were allowed to do evals and the TA attempted to rush that process so as to smother the anticipated negative reviews, the class came together as one to complete them and tell UCLA to dump her.

If you go to lecture, you do not need to read most of the readings she posts. Just go to class and you will get a good grade. Also, be active in class. She loves it when people engage in conversation during lecture.
Goes on tangents so you don't know what's important. Also tough grader; on the midterm the highest grade was a C-. Luckily she grades on a curve.

her tests are very tricky and go into the slightest detail of the book. You have to read and understand/memorize the entire book and graphs. test averages were midterm 1 73% midterm 2 63%. Final?

You must read the book ahead in order to do those quizzes. She wants the student to succeed, and helps a lot during office hour.

She's a good teacher, but you definitely need to read the book and listen to her lecture pod casts. It is a difficult class - you should spend at least four hours per lecture studying. It is very interesting though and she tells cool stories about the subject.

Cares about her students. Her exams are some of the hardest I've ever taken. Do the homework and reading before class. Class consisted purely of clicker questions for practice, you must come in already knowing the material. Discussion is boring, all simulations and a quick walk through the botanical garden.

If you have no programming experience (like me), his class is very hard. If you do (like many of the people in the class), you probably won't need much effort in this class.

Extremely helpful! I was in actually both his CS31 and CS32 classes, and Dr. Smallberg was very clear and precise about everything he taught. I emailed him on average 10 emails per proj/hw, and he would patiently answer all of them, and I'm just ONE of about 200 of his students! The only prof that would top his teaching style is probably Nachenberg
Okay prof for teaching, but if you really want a good lecturer, take Nachenburg, but you can go to w/e com sci teacher, the hw and basic material for the classes are the same

Take good notes, he has no power point and lectures like a stream of consciousness. Write fast and study hard to pass. Tough on grading, you really need to know the answers. Very interesting and funny lectures.
It's difficult to understand what he says.

I took Professor Phillips Fall 2020 & loved his class. His class is Asynchronous & lecture heavy. You don't really need to read bc everything is in the lectures

Professor Diaconecsu is an amazing woman. She is not only a profound researcher, but she is probably the most generous professors I have met. She is always willing to help. analysis.name: Larry Drasin
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

Written assignments don't match what's said to do in the videos, so there's some figuring out to do. Lots of the dates are wrong in the assignments too. And it's a lot of work, but is 6 credits, so…

Took DC1 with this professor and overall had a really awesome experience. Was extremely accommodating with deadlines and very helpful for feedback. The instructor is not very tech savvy, however. There was often repeated lecture videos within the same module from different years, wrong assignment deadlines, and incorrect turning in instructions.

Larry is such a good professor! He explains everything so thoroughly in his lessons and is very clear about grading criteria and feedback. I never had any questions about how to complete assignments or what was expected. My class was remote/asynchronous but he recorded individualized videos reviewing our work every week which was so helpful.

Larry is great, he is very helpful and thorough. He will constantly remind you that this is a technical course, not an art class so we should focus on perfection of our technical abilities. I love that because I get caught up with details and waste time a lot so time management is key here. I've developed so many skills, I hope to take his DC2&DC3.

Larry is very direct, even blunt. But I definitely got a lot out of the course which is more than I could say about DC I. Take notes, he likes you to take notes. I did not get the impression that he was handing out grades. I feel I'm in the minority getting an A+.

analysis.name: Daniel Fessler

He's that rare combination of brilliance and kindness. His teaching style is excellent. He'll always stick out in my mind as a professor I was incredibly lucky to have. You'll learn a lot about literature, the United States' history and society, and yourself in a class of Yarborough's. Do take one if you have the chance.

analysis.name: Teo Ruiz
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 33 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Ruiz has been in National Geographic television for the show "TABOO" and was a Princeton University professor in his earlier years. His lectures are so moving, he will almost bring tears to your eyes. He incorporates history and life as a human being into great depth, touching the heart of a student who truly loves knowledge and learning.
Love him. Insisted we call him God.

The first lecture I attended Freshman year. After stepping out of Moore, I thought all professors were as enthusiastic, bright spirited and passionate in what they taught. He has a great sense of humor and truly cares about his students. I regret not going to office hours, yet I will definitely take another class with him. Take him! Easy A as well.

If there were words to describe Teo! He likes you to show up to class but I wouldn't necessarily call it mandatory, but it gets to the point where you WANT to show up to class. He really is a storyteller and sucks you in in lecture to the point where you don't realize you learned anything until you write a paper. Incredible teacher and human being!
teo is the greatest! do urself some good and take a class with teo. went to paris with him this past summer...a truly amazing experience!

analysis.name: Seana Shiffrin
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very disappointing experience. I enrolled late due to waitlists and was met with immediate frustration from the professor, who said she was unhappy and suggested I shouldn't have joined. Despite explaining my situation and having an IEP, I felt dismissed and unsupported. The class environment felt tense and unwelcoming, I was belittled and unwelcom

terrible disposition. She is condescending & rude to students,outside and inside class. However, her lectures are crystal clear and the subject material is fascinating. It's a crime her personality isn't as remarkable as her pedagogy.  She is quite brilliant. Genius, in fact. TERRIBLE PROFESSOR AS A WHOLE!! TERRIBLE!!

Shiffrin can be cold.  However, I've also experienced how helpful she can be.  I don't know what I did or didn't do to receive the help I did from her, but she's actually quite compassionate.  If you want to be challenged, take her classes.  I took normative ethics and Rawls.  She is definitely something else.

Professor Shiffrin is one of the most intelligent people I have ever met. While this sometimes makes her intimidating, she truly cares about her students and makes every effort to make sure they're understanding the material and are engaged. Excellent professor.
Great lecturer. And she's very helpful during office hours, but beware...if you are talking nonsense she'll call you out. She also teaches at the Law school.

analysis.name: Terence Tao
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: It's just plain wonderful to be taught by the smartest, most amicable mathematician alive! Truly amazing.
Amazing professor, this guy is literally a genius. It's amazing to learn from him.
Best professor i have ever met.
Hes absolutely awful. He talks really fast and expects you to comprehend everything
Lightest class of my life, would 100% take again for the free 100, Terrance Tao ngl the GOAT.

analysis.name: Thomas Sherrer
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Sherrer is honestly a great professor who understands students have a lot on their plate. The class consisted of two homework assignments, a midterm and a final which are open notes. Attendance isn't mandatory and he is super helpful and posts recordings and slides of all of his lectures to reference. His class was an easy A and he's great.
He is sweet, personally I found the exams pretty challenging though. They are open note so that is a plus.
Honestly, if you ARENT taking Sherrer, I don't know what you are doing. Dude literally lets you use hand-written notes on tests and curves his exams. PS he is also rlly good looking

analysis.name: Albion Urdank
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Sitting through his boring lectures are beyond painful.

I hated this teacher, he was not only boring but he took very simple ideas and dragged it out to the point where I was confused and didnt even know what we were taking about. He also can seem to humiliate his students. He also took roll 5 minutes before class started and for all of us coming from other classes were late though his watch is fast

One of the best and nicest professors at UCLA.  There's a midterm, a short(5-7 pages)"thought piece" paper, and a final.  All the questions are straightforward; there are no tricks.  Lectures are very informative and interesting and the books he assigns very interesting.  Highly recommended.

As a high school summer studies student I really enjoyed the class. I found Prof. Urdank's lectures interesting. Although depending upon which midterm and final essays were chosen, there was no need to buy all the texts and this should have been made clearer at the start of the quarter.

Prof. Urdank took a jockish, boorish, apathetic student and inspired a love of History.  That was me back in the late-80s. He's really laid back, but his methods are Socratic, so be prepared when you get into class or be prepared to shrink down into your chair.  My favorite prof. at UCLA, and still going strong

analysis.name: Charlene Villaseñor-Black
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
It is important to address the issues that are affecting our community today. Our parents and grandparents worked hard for us so we can enjoy the privileges we have today in the academic world. 
Some profs. were given a chance under the Diversity, Equity and Inclusion program. Charlene was one of those bc she is a Chicano female of indigent roots.

She really cares about the chicano community.  We are sure that she will take a strong leadership role in  addressing the challenges in our city. Our parents and grandparents have worked a lot for their children and grandchildren and deserve the support of those who by pure luck are doing better and making a lot of money.  Please talk to her asap.

When we come back in the fall, Charlene will discuss the  issues affecting the Los Angeles community these days. She is the leader of The New Aztlan and chair of Chicano Studies. She is giving a voice to the oppressed at UCLA
 Please support her brave endeavors

I agree with the opinions of fellow students. She is one of the best teachers in the department.
It is a role model for many students who come from disadvantaged backgrounds. The thing to do these days is to apply for scholarships with major foundations like Rockefeller, Ford or Wilson. Those are the best avenues to undo the oppressive thoughts.

Liked the course very much, though it is a lot of work. Charlene's projects are motivating. She is involved in the history of art and the interpretation of decolonizing the minds of the Chicana /o people. Please follow her lead and apply for the Woodrow Wilson Foundation scholarship. It is the accurate path to decolonization of the oppressed

analysis.name: Victor Bascara
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Bascara's writing prompts are REALLY confusing. The papers are all pretty long (6-7 pages) and the prompts can be a full page long without clearly stating what he wants or asking a direct question. He also sprung a last minute paper on us in wk 10 with an unclear prompt. The readings are great & hopefully your TA can decode the prompts in section.

Although Prof. Bascara has a very different style of teaching, he is a fantastic professor nonetheless.  There is a lot of reading but also lots of group assignments and stuff.  Papers are definitely doable and actually interesting.  I would totally recommend him and I'm not an Asian Studies major.  He's incredibly nice/helpful! Take him!!!

Prof. Bascara is a real gem. He keeps class interesting with a variety of teaching strategies, films, and active-learning activities. He inspires us to think critically about the world and make the change that we want to see. He is accessible outside of class and gives feedback that is both helpful and motivational. Hands down the best!
Prof. Bascara is hot!! But yeah, a good teacher also.. take him. you'll enjoy him.

analysis.name: Vinay Lal
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: His lectures are very fast. He doesn't allow time for questions. His grader is ridiculously unfair. Lal claims there's a 10% portion for attendance, which he doesn't take, yet I never missed a class and my final grade never changed. Be prepared to read several novels and an excruciating amount of dense material. It's an unrealistic load of reading.

Very interesting and informative. He recognizes his own bias, and attempts to give both sides. Discussions are mandatory and TAs check to make sure you go to lecture. You should go to lecture because that is the main source of information. Midterms alright, final is difficult but doable. Skim the readings, but GO TO LECTURE!

Professor Lal's lectures are engaging, weaving in his opinions (w/o leaving out other POVs), anecdotes and interesting insights into how the material is relevant today. History, as he says, is the study of the future. You'll study history for ideas, not rote facts. The readings may be a bit heavy esp. if history is not your favorite subject.

Profesor LAL is one of the best History instructors ever. He is an intellectual and challenges you like mad balls. He expects his undergrads to work like graduate students. If you are a lazy loafer, don't take any of his classes. However, if your up for a
He's pretty cool. He's a genius.

analysis.name: Paul Von Blum
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 36 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Itrs professors like Von Blum that make UCLA prestigious and worthy of anything. He's been in the UC system I believe for over 20 years begining with Berkeley.. part of the Civil Rights movement.. I cant say more than passionate & experienced to describe him. You must take a class with him to make your education worth anything!

Who takes you on a field trip to LA county Jail for a Speech class - Prof. Von Blum. This man poseses a gift for teaching. He inspires and excites his students through the greatest lessons of all, that which come from real life experience. I'll never forget some of the discussions we had in and out of class. How many teachers truly make that kind of an impact? I hope he'd take pride knowing he's partialy to blame for the passion (and stories) I bring into my own classroom.

I took several of his pol sci classes at UC Irvine 20 yrs ago and thought so highly of him that I recently looked him up to see if he was still at UCLA.  He challenged us with moral and ethical scenarios which made us think and some didn't like it.  I loved it and thought he was very brave to push the envelope on taboo subjects.BEST Teacher EVER!

I took courses with Paul Von Blum at U.C. Berkeley over 35 years ago.  He was a highlight of my college experience.  I still think about the books we read (The Plague, Miss Lonelyhearts, The Loneliness of the Long Distance Runner, etc.) as well as the artists we studied (Hogarth, Goya, Daumier, etc.)  Thank you, Paul, for everything!

I disagree with the previous review. My discussion sections with my TA helped glue everything from lecture and readings together. I did very well in the class and found my TA to be very accessible and helpful. If you write well and convincingly, participate in discussion, and attend sections, you'll get an easy A.

analysis.name: Brian Van Koten
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Brian a smart guy, and his lectures and written homeworks force you to learn a lot. Unfortunately, the written homeworks are a lot of work, and they generally don't match what you do on the quizzes. He also doesn't give clear feedback on quizzes, and the class felt disorganized. Our grades were not posted online, and deadlines were always changing.

Professor Van Koten is a young, new professor. He is clear with his examples and he's always willing to answer questions. His exams, however, are very difficult. He's more focused on proofs . His class is kind of demanding since he has quizzes every week. It was the first time I struggled in a math class. I worked very hard and ended up with a B.

He teaches well and has good examples. The problem is his tests. The av. on the midterm/final was about 50%. I got a C+ which is super disappointing because I've never gotten below an A in math ever before. His tests are SUPER hypothetical, confusing, and downright almost impossible to pass. May the curve be ever in your favor.
Van Koten is a new professor, and he is very clear with his examples. He focuses on theory and proofs, rather than routine calculation. If you do the homework in his class, you will be fine

analysis.name: Lothar Von Falkenhausen
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: His accent is fine, the subject is demanding so don't no slackers need apply. The more I read up in this subject, the more I find his name cross referenced everywhere! He's an expert for sure and can answer any question your throw at him provided it's relevant. Difficult, but you'll learn so much. That's why were here after all right?

He is extremely well-educated and provides ample background on everything he teaches, going beyond dry facts. His attention to detail and knowledge of entertaining tidbits of history make for a very stimulating class. You write a lot but he is not a hard grader at all, and very encouraging. He is enthusiastic + informed. He made me a better writer.

Some students felt that the thick German accent and esoteric nature of his teaching were difficult to understand.  But he was one of my favorite teachers, who got beyond the boring art-historical information and went into symbolism.
Personally found the subjects provided mind numbing.  Maybe it is his heavy accent, but I didn't feel like he engaged the class to participate in conversation/discussion of presented material
Hes a very good professor! Amazing class! I feel like I am learning more than being stressed about silly exams.

analysis.name: Liam Watson
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He gives 6 long tough assignments instead of a final but you're allowed to work with others and get help during office hours. If you like the material and are comfortable with proofs then it's gonna be a great time cause Liam is very enthusiastic about the topic and it shows.

Very chill teaching style and super reasonable guy. Frequently checked in with the class to make sure things were going well and that he wasn't going too fast or too slow. We had 6 assignments that made up our entire grade which was a welcomed change of pace for me. He gives lots of help on the assignments if you ask for it.
Professor Watson is at UBC Math dept now and is an excellent professor. Great humour, very polite, quick to get back to you. Just all around excellent asset for UBC.
Professor Watson is really talented at presenting lectures. And his exams are nice and closely related to the course materials. I had a great time having his class.

analysis.name: Jae Yoon

review_highlights.comment: I don't know what the other comment is talking about I don't think i've ever seen professor yoon be anything other than friendly. The class was a good pace and the lectures were always interesting because she's good at adding personal anecdotes here and there. The only thing is the tests are a bit hard but straightforward if you study everything.
Mrs Yoon is nice but sometime very strict and cranky or moody. This class was all about memorization. If you are a native Korean speaker you will do fine. Overall it was fun.
Very helpful.Willing to listen and work with you.Highly recommend.

analysis.name: Yan Shen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Lovely and caring lady but very intense Chinese course. She teaches the class almost entirely in Mandarin and covers a ton of material. It's very fast-paced... You will learn a lot but will fall easily behind if you don't keep up. The exams are brutal. Make sure your schedule is flexible and dedicate much time to studying otherwise you will burn.

Yan lao shi is amazing! She is so caring and helpful. Her lectures are all in Mandarin and she pushes you to speak as much Chinese as possible, so you have to study a lot in order to keep up. Exams are difficult but not impossible, and quizzes are the hardest part of the class, but she drops your two lowest quiz scores. Amazing teacher overall!
Shen laoshi is an inspiration. She cares about her students more than any teacher I've ever met and is so wholesome and kind. I learned so much and never felt that I wouldn't be able to suceed.

analysis.name: Yogita Goyal

review_highlights.comment: WONDERFUL! Clear, organized, prepared, she makes class a breeze. The books were great and her lectures were heavenly. Study guides match the tests so easy As. Great to talk to in office hours, great to do honors with, GREAT PERSON. She was concerned for our learning and the whole class felt appreciated and respected by her.

Professor Goyal is extremely knowledgeable about African American History. Always available during office hours, she genuinely cares about students. She is a fair grader and gives great feedbacks. She is brilliant and her passion for the topic is contagious.I loved her class and style of teaching.
I know that Prof Goyal yells at department staff, showing elitism and poor character.
She is a great, extremely intelligent teacher.  However, it is essential one keeps decorum and writes well in order to succeed in her class.

She hate females. Incredibly boring and opinionated. Many of us met after class for "therapy" to decompress because she is an awful person. Thank God I will never have to take her again, could not pay me enough. Her criticism is not helpful or contractive. Does not lend itself to improving. Take only if you are a masochist.

analysis.name: Craig Yirush
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Yirush is very challenging, but he makes you a better writer while teaching history in an organized, logical, but interesting and fun way. His use of primary sources is incredibly beneficial, and he essentially provides you with all the evidence you need for your papers. Again, he is tough, but his criticism of your writing is truly a gift.

Yirush is great! Super helpful in office hours. His enthusiasm for early American history is infectious. Get ready to read a LOT every week, 150 pages a week of dense primary source material is not unusual. But if you enjoy American history you will love his class, one of my favorite profs at UCLA

I enjoyed this class alot. Professor Yirush really knows the subject and is passionate about it. There is a lot of class discussion and he likes to call on the unsuspecting (always be prepared). Lots of memorizing dates and reading from primary sources. I would definitely take his class again.

One of the toughest classes I've ever had, but it was worth it because it made me a better writer.  It also made me better at analyzing primary sources.  He pushes you to give him your very best, although if you're looking to help your GPA more than your writing skills take someone else.
Yirush is an excellent lecturer - kept things interesting and fast paced.  He is tough but fair & consistent.  I would highly recommend this Professor.
